next: fall back to open repo issues when board is empty

When the project board has no actionable items, check for open issues

when there are open issues not yet on the board.

commit.message:
fix(dx): scoring script bug fixes + re-run against all 6 pilot conditions

Fixes applied:
1. grep -> Node.js fs scan (cross-platform, no shell grep dependency)
2. --passWithNoTests removed; test count checked explicitly (0 tests = fail)
3. Coverage parsed from jest --coverage output; threshold 60% on feature file
4. rm -rf -> fs.rmSync(recursive) (cross-platform cleanup)
5. git clone --depth 1 -> --depth 20 (commit history check sees real history)
6. Bounded: checks new feature route only (digest.ts / activity.ts), not all routes

Re-run results (corrected gradient):
  naive:      1/7 automatable (scaffold commit artifact; real forks expected 0)
  competent:  3/7
  gs:         7/7 (perfect on all automatable checks)

GS achieves ceiling on automatable checks. Composable +3 (live server) is the
only remaining gap before April session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add performance metrics to controllers (#499)

* Initial plan

* Add performance metrics (reconcile/create/delete duration) to controllers

Add Prometheus histogram metrics to track:
- GameServer controller reconciliation duration
- GameServerBuild controller reconciliation duration
- Individual GameServer creation duration
- Individual GameServer deletion duration

Co-authored-by: dgkanatsios <8256138+dgkanatsios@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/PlayFab/thundernetes/sessions/7e3c4e94-d118-43dd-9d35-d93f02f2f5c5

* Remove per-operation creation/deletion duration metrics

Individual K8s API calls for GameServer creation/deletion are very fast,
so per-operation duration metrics would be close to zero and not useful.
The reconciliation duration histograms already capture the full picture
including all batch creation/deletion time.

commit.message:
feat(dx): add self-administered pilot results — baseline scores for April experiment

Six conditions piloted across both workshop repos:
- base-naive / kanban-naive: 0/14, Prisma in all routes, no tests
- base-competent / kanban-competent: 2-3/14, tests passing, no architecture
- base-gs / kanban-gs: 6-8/14, service layer, CLAUDE.md, ADRs, passing tests

Scoring script issues found (4 bugs, 2 calibration issues):
- Bounded check is a false positive on Windows (grep unavailable)
- Verifiable false positive when 0 tests + --passWithNoTests flag
- Coverage check not enforced in automation
- git --depth 1 makes commit format check unreliable

Rubric calibration: Bounded check fails for GS because pre-existing scaffold
routes carry Prisma. New feature routes (digest.ts, activity.ts) are clean.
Recommendation: check Bounded on new/changed files only.

All implementations in experiments/dx/pilot/. Results in results.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): add §7.8 Meta-Application — autonomous specification evolution

New section covering:
- AX experiment as simultaneous ForgeCraft development mechanism
- close_cycle audit loop: AI identifies gap, AI encodes fix, human validates
- Human role as trigger/validator, not author (git history verifiable)
- Eventually convergent (not monotone) — v4 regression explained as gap interaction
- F3 dependency rebuttal: ForgeCraft 1.0 emerged from AX, not prior to it
- Self-applicability as strongest implication: pragmatic tier governs its own instrument

Cross-references: §4.2 (pragmatic tier), §7.7 (F3 threat), §9.1 (agentic self-refinement), §10 (community ratchet)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

argument applies — as an untested hypothesis — to any domain' (§10). Added

build: declare Python 3.10 support (#12)

* build: declare Python 3.10 support

Lower the declared minimum Python version to 3.10, add 3.10 to the CI test matrix, and align the docs and release workflows with the same supported floor.

Python 3.8 remains a separate follow-up because the current codebase depends on dataclass slots support and inspect.get_annotations().

Refs: #11

commit.message:
a11y: Improve WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA compliance with focus & contrast fixes

Accessibility improvements addressing keyboard navigation and color contrast:

1. Icon contrast (WCAG 1.4.11 AA) — Replace low-contrast icon colors:
   - text-gray-400 (4.5:1) → text-gray-700 (10.4:1) in:
     • layouts/partials/activity/booking.html (close icon, calendar icon)
     • layouts/partials/activity/product_info.html (duration, persons icons)
     • layouts/activities/list.html (dropdown arrow icon)

2. Keyboard focus indicators (WCAG 2.4.7 AA):
   - Replace anti-pattern focus:outline-none in activities/li.html
   - Use proper focus-visible pattern: outline-2 outline-offset-2 outline-primary
   - Ensures visible focus when navigating with keyboard

3. Navigation focus (WCAG 2.1.1 A):
   - Add focus-visible patterns to sticky nav links (About, Practical Info, Gallery)
   - Add focus-visible patterns to tag links in activity hero sections
   - Use outline-white for tag links (colored backgrounds)

All changes maintain visual design while improving accessibility compliance.
Build verified: 71 pages generated successfully with 0 errors.

commit.message:
fix: migration 035 — drop trigger before dropping username column

PostgreSQL cannot drop a column if a trigger references it.
Migration 011 created users_username_not_reserved trigger and
check_username_not_reserved() function bound to the username column.
Drop both before ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN.

fix(deploy): run build:esm alongside build in Step 1

The ESM output (dist/esm/) is what the CDN serves, but deploy.sh only
ran `npm run build` (CJS). Any export added to src/index.ts after the
last ESM build would be missing from the CDN-delivered file.

Now both builds run together so dist/esm/ is always in sync before the
artifacts are committed and tagged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: treat Geant4 kInfinity as no-intersection in TraceRay (#255)

Geant4's kInfinity (CLHEP) is 9.9999e+99, a large but finite double.
std::isinf() returns false for it, so rays where DistanceToIn returns
kInfinity (no intersection) were being treated as valid hits.

This caused SurfaceNormal to be evaluated at kInfinity * direction,
a nonsensical point far outside the solid, producing garbage normals
and false surface-normal mismatches.  For G4TwistedTubs (dphi=120°),
this accounted for all 1381 reported surface-normal failures.

Fix:
- DistanceString: also print "kInfinity" for values >= kInfinity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: G4OCCT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: rebuild ESM dist to include PositionManager and missing exports

The dist/esm/index.js was stale — it was not rebuilt after PositionManager,
withObserver, ObserverMixin, log/warn, and related types were added to
src/index.ts. The CDN delivery path (dist/esm/) was therefore missing these
exports, causing a SyntaxError at runtime for any consumer importing
PositionManager from the CDN URL.

Add NetHost discovery and HostFxr.Discovery() method (#2)

* Add NetHost discovery and HostFxr.Discovery() method

Replace inline dotnet root discovery in HostFxr static constructor with
NetHost.Discover(), a managed implementation of the native nethost
component's get_hostfxr_path algorithm.

Discovery searches in order:
- Architecture-specific env vars (DOTNET_ROOT_ARM64, etc.)
- Generic DOTNET_ROOT env var
- Registered install locations (/etc/dotnet/install_location on Unix)
- PATH-based discovery with symlink resolution
- Well-known default locations

Also rename Main() to HostFxrMain() to avoid entry point conflicts
when consumed by projects using top-level statements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README for NetHost discovery API

commit.message:
fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture (#253)

* fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture

G4ScaledSolid::DistanceToOut (Geant4 bug, present through at least 11.4.1)
calls fScale->TransformNormal() on the exit normal returned by the unscaled
base solid.  TransformNormal maps normals from global→local frame, but the
base-solid normal (solNorm) is already in the local (unscaled) frame.  The
correct transform is InverseTransformNormal() (local→global), which applies
the inverse-transpose of the scale matrix (divide each component by its
scale factor).  G4ScaledSolid::SurfaceNormal() uses InverseTransformNormal()
correctly, explaining why SurfaceNormal yields 0 mismatches while
DistanceToOut exit normals show 2039/2048 failures for the non-uniformly
scaled sphere fixture (semi-axes 15×20×5 mm).

Fix in G4OCCT: introduce G4ScaledSolidFixed, a minimal subclass that
overrides DistanceToOut to recompute the exit normal via SurfaceNormal()
at the exit point.  SurfaceNormal() is correctly implemented in Geant4,
so this gives the geometrically correct outward normal without any
modification to the Geant4 source tree.  The fixture builder now constructs
G4ScaledSolidFixed instead of G4ScaledSolid for native-solid comparisons.

Remove the broad G4ScaledSolid xfail in fixture_validation.cc (reason was
stale: 'not implemented yet').  Add a comment explaining the Geant4 bug and
the workaround.  Document the bug and fix strategy in provenance.yaml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Update src/tests/fixtures/geometry/wrapper-decorator/G4ScaledSolid/scaled-sphere-nonuniform-v1/provenance.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reimplement G4ScaledSolidFixed::DistanceToOut with InverseTransformNormal (#254)

* Initial plan

* fix: reimplement G4ScaledSolidFixed::DistanceToOut using InverseTransformNormal directly

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/08725f2b-a34a-48d0-9b6d-93908f9c13a9

* fix: use GetScaleTransform/GetUnscaledSolid public accessors instead of private members

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.1.59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(Portainer): modular compose files + add .env.essential

Task 1: Reorganize Portainer/ directory
- DELETE old monolithic docker-compose-full.yml (1336 lines) and
  docker-compose-minimal.yml (372 lines)
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-postgres.yml — Portainer-specific version
  that uses the base image directly (no build context) and installs locale
  via entrypoint
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-group-minimal.yml — includes Portainer
  postgres + reuses parent redis & ragflow
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-group-databases.yml — includes Portainer
  postgres + reuses parent redis, qdrant, neo4j, minio
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-full.yml — full stack using include with
  relative paths; only postgres is Portainer-specific

Task 2: Create .env.essential
- Contains only required variables (secrets, passwords, API keys)
- Template with CHANGE_ME placeholders for all secrets
- Optional SMTP section at the end

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: sanitize template-rendered data and add sanitizeBody() (#48)

Add sanitizeBody() that preserves newlines/tabs for <pre> blocks while
stripping control and BiDi characters. Register sanitize and sanitizeBody
in template.FuncMap and apply pipes to all 12 untrusted template expressions
across 6 template files.

Defense-in-depth: pre-sanitize error data in 7 route handlers via new
sanitizeErr() helper before passing to templates. Fix auth.go login error
from URL query param (was unsanitized).

Tests: TestSanitizeBody (30 cases), TestSanitizeErr (4 cases),
TestTemplateFuncMapRegistration, TestTemplateSanitizeConditional (4 cases).

Closes #47

Co-authored-by: Marius Sutara (from Dev Box) <marius@ntdev.microsoft.com>
